reviews.author: LIMA LIMA
reviews.text: I visited the Tomb of Minh Mang, the mausoleum of Minh Mang (r. 1820–1841), the second emperor of the Nguyen Dynasty. The perfect symmetry and symbolic layout are truly impressive. The architecture blends beautifully with the surrounding natural landscape, creating a peaceful and balanced atmosphere. While you can see influences from Ming and Qing imperial tombs, it still retains a distinctly Vietnamese aesthetic. A serene and thoughtfully designed site—definitely worth visiting in Hue.

reviews.author: Mr Ridan
reviews.text: This mausoleum is very spacious, with beautiful gardens, lakes, and traditional architecture. It feels more peaceful compared to some of the other tombs, and walking around gives you a good sense of history and nature combined. I visited on a day with pleasant weather and only light, short rain, which made the experience enjoyable and refreshing. The site is quite large, so be prepared to walk a bit, but the scenery is worth it. I used a combo ticket that included several sites in Hue, which made the visit good value. Overall, it’s a nice balance of culture, history, and nature – definitely worth including in your trip to Hue.

reviews.author: Janet
reviews.text: This one is the most impressive emperor tomb for me. I like its architecture, structure, atmosphere. I went there on a rainny day so there were not crowded. If I’m only able to go to one emperor tomb in Hue, I’ll pick this one

reviews.author: Kathleen Jeugmans
reviews.text: By the time you’ve wandered through Emperor Minh Mang’s graceful courtyards and crossed his perfectly symmetrical bridges, you’ll feel the calm weight of history wrap around you. Every path seems designed to impress both body and soul—though your legs might argue otherwise. It’s half royal dreamscape, half peaceful workout, and completely breathtaking in its balance of grandeur and serenity

reviews.author: Lachasso Nguyen
reviews.text: Absolutely beautiful and breath-taking. Every area was like stepping back into history. I paid for the audio tour guide but I don't think it was very worth it cuz it just had some description of the place, not much about the usage or purpose of that area. It was raining a bit so there was no other visitors there, soooo nice.

reviews.author: Uliana Pastushenko
reviews.text: I liked the place. It was really beautiful and aesthetically pleasing there. I’ve met some girls in national costumes taking pictures what made that place even more charming. interesting to explore, really worth the visit.

reviews.author: Artur Pytel
reviews.text: If you have been to the Citadel in the city center, you can skip this place, inside it is rather average. The tomb on the hill, number 9 on the attached map, is not even accessible. Better spend this money on Khai Dinh Tomb, especially that the price is the same.

reviews.author: I. Morgan
reviews.text: A stunning and peaceful site, beautifully set among lakes, gardens, and pine trees. The symmetry of the layout is impressive, and every gate, bridge, and pavilion feels perfectly placed. Built in 1843, the tomb reflects Emperor Minh Mang’s vision of harmony between architecture and nature. It’s a relaxing place to walk around, with plenty of shade and photo spots. Definitely one of Hue’s most scenic imperial tombs and well worth visiting.

reviews.author: Dave Hoang
reviews.text: A Serene and Majestic Heritage Landmark The Minh Mạng Tomb stands as a remarkable tribute to the splendor of the Nguyễn Dynasty. Nestled among verdant landscapes and tranquil lakes, it provides a serene escape from the pace of contemporary life. The architecture is both impressive and elegantly balanced, featuring intricately designed pavilions, temples, and bridges that harmonize with the natural setting. Impeccably preserved, the site invites visitors to enjoy peaceful walks along its scenic pathways. Rich in historical significance and visual beauty, it’s an essential stop for anyone exploring Huế. The courteous and well-informed staff further enrich the experience with valuable insights. A must-see for those with a passion for Vietnamese history and culture.

reviews.author: Reginald Williams
reviews.text: Unfortunately, our visit here was a real disappointment. It’s not hard to imagine (but we could only imagine) how this might be a lovely place to visit but within a five minute walk from the entrance all else was blocked from further view as all paths leading deeper in to the mausoleum’s premises were blocked by construction, and even near the entrance, little effort seemed to have been made to preserve what should be the serenity and beauty of the place. We strongly feel that this mausoleum should be closed to the public until construction and dredging are complete, or that the entrance fee should either be waived or drastically reduced.

reviews.author: Ara
reviews.text: Peaceful and Majestic The Tomb of Minh Mạng is one of the most impressive royal tombs in Huế. The architecture is stunning — perfectly symmetrical, with beautiful pavilions, lotus ponds, and quiet paths surrounded by nature. It feels peaceful and grand at the same time. A great place to learn about history and enjoy a calm, scenic walk. Definitely worth including on your Huế itinerary!

reviews.author: Andrew “Andy” Davis
reviews.text: Rented a bike and drive here. Near a beautiful lake and surrounded by water. It makes for a nice walk around. We came mid afternoon on a super hot day so needed to grab some drinks at the entrance and again on the exit. There is toilets and drinks and some food options here. We came on a week day. You pay to park a scooter but they don’t seem to charge locals but price is small. Not as big as we were led to believe but worth visiting.

reviews.author: Hung Nguyen
reviews.text: Its beautiful.The Tomb is covered with trees, lake and ancient buildings, which is very nice. You should pay for a tour guide. Our guide was a middle-aged man and he spoke so passionately and his knowledge of the place is amazing. One important note is that you should visit this tomb around 3 to 6 PM to avoid hot sun during the day. the sunset light is beautiful. Also noted that the whole place is open for visitors, but the tomb itself is forbidden. Only royal relatives can enter the tomb area once a year to pay tributes. Highly Recommended.

reviews.author: Matt Koob
reviews.text: This tomb was a wonderful display of the legacy of the Nguyen dynasty - showcasing the unique architecture, elaborate decorations, and connection to nature. The area has several buildings that are typical of the burial practices and worship of the Nguyen emperors - many of which are similar to those that can be found within the Imperial City/Citadel. The buildings have been restored to help showcase the grandeur, and to maintain the cultural/historical significance. Alongside most of the notable buildings, there is information on display (and possibly audio QR codes) in which visitors can learn a little bit about the history and importance of the buildings and surrounding area. The area also has some amazing natural scenery that exemplifies the beauty of this sacred space - pine forests, vibrant lakes, and well kept gardens allowed for visitors to step into another world.

reviews.author: Thach HUYNH
reviews.text: A Tranquil and Majestic Historical Site The Minh Mạng Tomb is a magnificent testament to the grandeur of the Nguyễn Dynasty. Set amidst lush greenery and serene lakes, the tomb offers a peaceful retreat from the hustle and bustle of modern life. The architecture is both grand and harmonious, with beautifully crafted pavilions, temples, and bridges that blend seamlessly with the natural surroundings. The site is meticulously maintained, and the pathways are perfect for a leisurely stroll. The historical significance of the tomb, combined with its stunning beauty, makes it a must-visit destination in Huế. The staff are courteous and knowledgeable, enhancing the overall experience with their insights. Highly recommended for anyone interested in Vietnamese history and culture.

reviews.author: JustLinahere
reviews.text: Such a solemn and majestic place. This imperial tomb is a blend of natural landscape and architectural complexes. Tomb was closed, only open once in a year on emperor's death anniversary. But one can see the rest. It took nearly one hour for us to visit the place. Restroom available.

reviews.author: Duy Tung Vu
reviews.text: One of the most beautiful royal tombs in Hue. Minh Mang’s Tomb features well-planned architecture that blends seamlessly with nature. Walking along the main axis from the entrance to the emperor’s burial site, the gently rising and falling terrain creates stunning, harmonious views with surprising depth. The path from the main temple to Minh Lau Pavilion — where the emperor once read books and admired the moon — is especially scenic. I recommend visiting in the morning or early afternoon for better light. I went late in the day, and while it was peaceful, the sunlight would have made the scenery even more vibrant. We hired a guide at the ticket counter (same price as an entry ticket), and it was worth it. The guide not only shared detailed stories but also showed deep pride and love for their heritage. Truly a meaningful experience.

reviews.author: Robert Chomicz
reviews.text: Beautiful grounds, made even better by the artificial waterways and the lack of crowds. When we were there maybe 20 others shared the site with us. It's a large tomb with several courts and many pavilions the gardens are tranquil and beautiful, the buildings taken care of though not restored

reviews.author: VIPUL TALWARIA
reviews.text: Our next stop was Minh Mang Tomb which is again in the outskirts of the city. This complex was fairly big and had a small pond/lake inside it. I loved the vibes of this place like where you can come just to chill. The place had multiple small structures. The entry fee for this is 150k VND.

reviews.author: Stephanie Tan
reviews.text: Quiet and tranquil place to relax. The structures are quite poorly maintained, and some sections are being restored, resulting in a very muddy pond. The tomb itself is closed to public and only opened once a year on the emperor's death anniversary.

reviews.author: Yamamoto Shohei
reviews.text: There are lots of picturesque spots. We were so satisfied and fascinated with great buildings. I recommend here for everyone visiting Hue. For us, it takes around 50 min to see around. After appreciating here, you can go to next tomb near here, Khai Dinh tomb. Including places Hanoi where I have been living since two years ago, this is one of my favorite place.

reviews.author: Khanh Linh
reviews.text: Great experience! I recommend you guys to go in the afternoon thus you do not have to wait too long to get a ticket. The deeper you go, the more beautiful the scenery becomes. If you go there in summer, esp from the end of May, June, July, do not forget to bring the suncreen, umbrella or hats cuz the sunlight is not a joke!

reviews.author: Do Cuong
reviews.text: Want to check out one of the most magnificent tombs in Hue? You should visit Minh Mang's Tomb! It was built for the second emperor of the Nguyen dynasty and was finished by his son, Emperor Thieu Tri. The tomb is huge and perfectly laid out with a main axis and symmetrical buildings. It's not just an impressive piece of architecture—it’s also a place that tells some really cool stories about the Nguyen dynasty.

reviews.author: Angie Fong
reviews.text: This was quite a impressive tomb with living area and all. Apparently the living area is for summer holidays with his favourite concubine for the season before his death. Nice.

reviews.author: Ian Kennedy
reviews.text: What a lovely place to visit. Very atmospheric even in occasionally heavy rain. It was much larger than I expected! You may be able to save some money by buying a ticket for multiple sites at once. We bought one that covered two tombs and the imperial palace in Hue for 420k, which was 80k less than buying the tickets separately.

reviews.author: Irene De la Torre Arenas
reviews.text: Absolutely stunning. It gets more and more beautiful the further you go. I’m not sure about the price of the ticket because we organized the visit with our hotel. We had a driver that took us to the different places which was very handy as some of the tombs are quite far

reviews.author: Chandra
reviews.text: This is the second tomb we visited on the tour. Unfortunately the main tomb area was closed when we went here, so we just walked around the area. Its so green and I liked walking around. Its also great for picture. The location to take good picture is dependent on time and where the sun shines

reviews.author: jr t
reviews.text: He was emperor from 1820-1840, the tomb site is wide with small lacs. Unfortunately fyou can’t see his tomb, the last door is closed.

reviews.author: Robin de Bruin
reviews.text: Balanced pathways under quiet, green trees. I noted how nearly 40 buildings stood in neat symmetry, blending into lakes, gardens, and hills around me. The main gate felt imposing and ceremonial, once opened only for the emperor’s remains. I passed through courtyards paved in ceramic tiles, past stone statues of mandarins and horses, toward a pavilion resting over a tranquil lake. The whole place felt peaceful and thoughtful—a place designed with care and purpose.

reviews.author: Adrian M
reviews.text: Probably my most favourite of the mausoleums we visited, offering a blend of traditional architecture structures set amongst nature that's beautiful and feels peaceful and serene. It's quite picturesque and we encountered quite a few people and even groups dressed up doing lots of poses for photographs. The layout of the grounds feels quite grand with arches, gates and bridges taking you through the different sections, across long paths giving you a sense you're somewhere important. It all leads towards the final burial place of the emperor which looks like a hill but in inaccessible. There's a couple of vendors out the front selling souvenirs, drinks and even espresso coffee. Inside, you can buy fish food to feed the koi in the ponds.
